prompt: This code does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: if(true) is not allowed by the Java compiler
Local variable x may not have been initialized; the compiler cannot prove x is always assigned before use
int variables must be declared with an initializer on the same line
System.out.println requires a String, not an int
explanation: The Java compiler performs definite-assignment analysis. Even though if(true) always executes, the compiler treats the branch as conditional. It cannot guarantee x is assigned, so it rejects the code. Fix: initialize x at declaration: int x = 0; or add an else branch.
